unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: In this unit, your child learns how we travel and get around. They meet the words for ways to travel (walk, bus, car, bicycle, boat) and learn to say how they go to school. They talk about journeys — going somewhere with the people they love, staying safe, and enjoying the trip. The goal is warm, confident English about the everyday world of going places — not perfect spelling.
unit.learningPath:
Begin with the guided teacher launch and learn a few words through pictures and play.
Listen to the Unit story and join in with repeated language.
Complete six language, speaking, early-writing and practical activities.
Meet your teacher three times each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question checkpoint and tell an adult what you can do.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Name common ways to travel (walk, bus, car, bicycle, boat, train, plane).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Say how they get to school: “I go to school by ___.”
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Say the action words for travel (drive, ride, fly, float, walk).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Use simple describing words for vehicles (big, little, fast, slow, and colours).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Join in the transport songs and rhymes, doing the actions.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Use short spoken and early-written English about transport, journeys and safe travel.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

readings.title: Amal's Big Bus Ride
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
Today, Amal was going to see her grandmother. Grandmother lived by the sea in a town far away.

“How will we get there?” asked Amal.

“First we walk. Then we go by bus,” said Mum. “Let's go!”

Selma and Mum walked down the road. They walked past the market. They walked to the bus stop.

Soon a big bus came. It was yellow and red. “Beep, beep!” went the bus.

“Find a seat. Then sit down,” said Mum. Amal found a seat by the window.

The wheels on the bus went round and round. The bus began to drive through the town.

“Look, Mum! A boy on a bicycle!” said Amal. The boy waved. His name was Adam.

“And look — a red car!” said Amal. The car went fast. But the bus went slow and bumpy.

Selma saw a girl named Samira, who was going to school. Samira waved too. Amal waved back.

The bus drove past the shops. It drove past the mosque. It drove all the way to the sea.

“We are here!” said Mum. At the sea, Amal saw a little blue boat. The boat could float on the water.

And there, by the boat, was Grandmother! “Amal!” she called. “You came to see me!”

“Yes!” said Amal. She ran and gave Grandmother a big hug.

“How did you travel here?” asked Grandmother.

“We walked, and then we came by bus!” said Amal. “The wheels went round and round!”

Grandmother smiled. “What a good journey. Now, shall we go on the boat?”

“Yes, yes!” said Amal. She held Grandmother's hand. “Let's go!”

readings.title: Talk about Let's Go!
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript: Look, point and talk. I go by ___. It goes on the ___. I can see a ___. The ___ is fast. We are ___ing. First we ___, then we ___. An adult reads each line while the learner points, repeats and acts.

readings.title: Let's Go! rhyme
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
“The Wheels on the Bus” — sing it together
The wheels on the bus go round and round,  /  round and round, round and round.  /  The wheels on the bus go round and round,  /  all day long!
Roll your hands round and round for the wheels. Then do more verses with actions: the wipers go “swish, swish, swish”, the bell goes “ding, ding, ding”. Sing it a little louder each time!
“Bus Driver, Bus Driver” — a little rhyme
Bus driver, bus driver,  /  may I have a ride?  /  Yes, of course.  /  Please come inside.  /  Find a seat.  /  Then sit down.  /  Buckle up.  /  We'll drive through the town.
Take turns: your child asks “May I have a ride?” and you answer. Pretend to find a seat, sit down and buckle up together.

grammar.title: Language pattern 1
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I go by ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: I go by ___.

grammar.title: Language pattern 2
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: It goes on the ___.

grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: It goes on the ___.

grammar.title: Language pattern 3
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I can see a ___.

grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: I can see a ___.

grammar.title: Language pattern 4
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: The ___ is fast.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: The ___ is fast.

grammar.title: Language pattern 5
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: We are ___ing.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: We are ___ing.

grammar.title: Language pattern 6
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: First we ___, then we ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: First we ___, then we ___.

speaking.title: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: I go by ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I go by ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 2 - Repeat the model
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: It goes on the ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern It goes on the ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I can see a ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - Picture talk
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: The ___ is fast. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern The ___ is fast. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Role-play
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: We are ___ing. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern We are ___ing. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - My confident turn
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: First we ___, then we ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern First we ___, then we ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

writing.title: Writing 1 - Draw and tell
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw one idea about transport, journeys and safe travel. Tell an adult what it shows.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 2 - Trace
writing.promptAndInstructions: Trace two useful words, saying each sound or word as you trace.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 3 - Match and copy

writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 4 - Label
writing.promptAndInstructions: Add two labels to your drawing or picture.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 6 - Create one sentence
writing.promptAndInstructions: Choose a picture and complete one short sentence using: First we ___, then we ___.

writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.

assignments.instructions: Submit one drawing or made object, two labels or traced words, one recorded spoken pattern and an adult-supported reflection.

quizzes.question: Which is a useful complete pattern for Let's Go!?
quizzes.options: I go by ___. | It goes on the ___. | I can see a ___. | The ___ is fast.
quizzes.explanation: The approved Unit pattern is: I go by ___.

quizzes.question: Which is a useful complete pattern for Let's Go!?
quizzes.options: It goes on the ___. | I go by ___. | I can see a ___. | The ___ is fast.
quizzes.explanation: The approved Unit pattern is: It goes on the ___.

quizzes.question: Which is a useful complete pattern for Let's Go!?
quizzes.options: I can see a ___. | I go by ___. | It goes on the ___. | The ___ is fast.
quizzes.explanation: The approved Unit pattern is: I can see a ___.

quizzes.question: Which is a useful complete pattern for Let's Go!?
quizzes.options: The ___ is fast. | I go by ___. | It goes on the ___. | I can see a ___.
quizzes.explanation: The approved Unit pattern is: The ___ is fast.

quizzes.question: Which is a useful complete pattern for Let's Go!?
quizzes.options: We are ___ing. | I go by ___. | It goes on the ___. | I can see a ___.
quizzes.explanation: The approved Unit pattern is: We are ___ing.

liveSessions.title: Meet the words
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring crayons and one real object connected to the Unit.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello song; 7 min model and repeat; 8 min game or story; 7 min guided practice; 3 min celebrate and preview
liveSessions.afterSession: Show one word, action, drawing or sentence from Let's Go! to an adult.

liveSessions.title: Story and talk
liveSessions.beforeSession: Complete the previous short practice with an adult.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello song; 7 min model and repeat; 8 min game or story; 7 min guided practice; 3 min celebrate and preview
liveSessions.afterSession: Show one word, action, drawing or sentence from Let's Go! to an adult.

teacherNotes.note: Use 15-20 minute independent blocks inside the lesson. Read every instruction aloud, model before expecting a response, and accept pointing, acting, drawing, tracing or speaking as valid evidence.
A teacher or caregiver remains present whenever the young learner uses recording or AI tutor features. The adult controls the device and protects the child's personal information.

selfAssessment.statement: I can name common ways to travel (walk, bus, car, bicycle, boat, train, plane).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say how they get to school: “I go to school by ___.”.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say the action words for travel (drive, ride, fly, float, walk).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can use simple describing words for vehicles (big, little, fast, slow, and colours).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can join in the transport songs and rhymes, doing the actions.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can use short spoken and early-written English about transport, journeys and safe travel.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

rubrics.level4: Uses a capital and end mark independently
